title: euraphis squarrosa
intro: Euraphis squarrosa, also known as Rough-barked Euraphis, is a small shrub native to eastern Australia, characterized by its woody stem and small, white flowers. The medicinal properties of Euraphis squarrosa are attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and possessing antimicrobial properties. Euraphis squarrosa can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory ailments.
